article.title: Final Fantasy XIV Reveals Evercold for January 2027 With a Twin-Mode Battle System Overhaul, an Evangelion Raid, and the Game's First New Saga in 13 Years
article.summary: At Fan Festival 2026 in Anaheim, Square Enix announced FFXIV's sixth expansion Evercold, a Switch 2 launch in August, Reborn and Evolved combat modes, and an Evangelion alliance raid co-developed with khara, Inc.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Square Enix used the keynote of Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival 2026 in Anaheim to unveil the MMO's sixth expansion, *Evercold*, alongside a battery of changes that producer and director Naoki Yoshida framed as the largest structural shift since *A Realm Reborn*. The expansion is scheduled to launch in January 2027 and will open the *Godless Realms Saga*, [the first new long-form story arc in the game's 13-year history according to Kotaku](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-expansion-ff14-trailer-release-2000690538). Square Enix paired the reveal with two other major announcements: a Nintendo Switch 2 version arriving in August 2026, and the eventual end of PlayStation 4 support midway through *Evercold*'s patch cycle.

## What Was Announced

*Evercold* is patch 8.0 and will raise the level cap from 100 to 110, while adding two new non-limited jobs — a tank and a physical ranged DPS — both original to *FFXIV* rather than returning classes from older Final Fantasy titles. [Kotaku reports](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-ffxiv-jobs-raids-2000690625) that the specific jobs will be revealed individually at the upcoming Fan Festivals in Berlin and Tokyo, and that a separate limited job, Beastmaster, will arrive earlier in Patch 7.56.

The most consequential gameplay change is a new dual-mode combat system. Every one of the 21 existing non-limited jobs will be playable in either **Reborn Mode**, which preserves the current rotations and skill sets, or **Evolved Mode**, which reworks each job around streamlined inputs and what the developers describe as stronger "job identity." [According to Kotaku's breakdown](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-ffxiv-jobs-raids-2000690625), players can swap between the two modes freely outside combat without changing gear or level, and both will remain viable in high-end content. Future jobs added after *Evercold* will only ship with Evolved Mode.

Square Enix also confirmed an alliance raid series titled *EVANGELION — Ghosts of Desire*, a 24-player crossover developed in collaboration with anime studio khara, Inc. Yoshida, who described himself in the keynote as "a massive *Evangelion* otaku," [told the Anaheim audience that the partnership had been years in the making, per Kotaku](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-ffxiv-jobs-raids-2000690625).

The expansion's setting is a new dimension the game calls the Fourth — a frozen reflection of the world the Warrior of Light must visit to keep an encroaching ice storm from consuming it. [Kotaku's coverage of the teaser trailer](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-expansion-ff14-trailer-release-2000690538) notes that the previous decade-plus of *FFXIV* content, from *A Realm Reborn* through *Dawntrail*, formed a single overarching narrative; *Evercold* is the first true reset of that frame.

## Switch 2 in August, PS4 Out by Patch 8.3

Square Enix CEO Takashi Kiryu took the stage to announce that *Final Fantasy XIV* will arrive on Nintendo Switch 2 in August 2026, preceded by a free one-month early access period to load-test servers on the new hardware. [Kotaku reports](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-switch-2-subscription-ff14-2000690550) that during the live demo Yoshida ran around the iconic Limsa Lominsa plaza on a Switch 2 unit on stage, a moment Yoshida said had "kind of freaked out" Nintendo when he insisted on showing it live.

The Switch 2 release comes with an unusual subscription wrinkle. Players will need to buy the Switch 2 client separately and pay a separate *FFXIV* subscription tied to that platform, [Kotaku reports](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-switch-2-subscription-ff14-2000690550); existing subscribers on PC, PlayStation, or Xbox can get the Switch 2 sub at half price, but the costs do not merge. Notably, a Nintendo Switch Online membership is not required to play, which differs from the model Microsoft applies to Xbox. The Kotaku piece characterizes the dual-subscription requirement as the result of "many months of discussion with Nintendo."

On the platform-retirement side, [Kotaku quotes Yoshida](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-ffxiv-jobs-raids-2000690625) telling the audience: "If you're still playing on a PS4, you've got two years to save up for a PS5." Square Enix plans to drop PS4 support after Patch 8.3, midway through *Evercold*'s lifecycle, citing the expansion's growing install size as the trigger.

## Other Changes Coming With 8.0

The announcement included several quality-of-life and structural updates: data-center regional boundaries on North American servers will be removed so that players across regions can party freely, character creation gains a color-wheel-based picker for skin, hair, and eye color, and the game introduces a new "Seasons" system tied to Patch 8.0. [Kotaku notes](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-evercold-ffxiv-jobs-raids-2000690625) that an armory overhaul and expanded customization options were also flagged in the keynote.

## What We Don't Know

Square Enix has not disclosed pricing for the *Evercold* collector's editions, the specific January 2027 release day, or the names of the two new jobs — those will be parceled out across the Berlin and Tokyo Fan Festivals later in 2026. The studio also did not commit to dates for the *Evangelion — Ghosts of Desire* raid tiers, which typically ship across an expansion's patch cycle rather than at launch.

## Why It Matters

*Final Fantasy XIV* is one of the few legacy MMOs whose subscriber base has continued to grow over the last decade, and *Evercold* arrives at a moment when the genre's incumbents are under pressure. The combination of a story-arc reset, a battle-system fork that effectively gives every existing job two playable identities, and a launch on Nintendo's newest hardware represents the most aggressive structural change Square Enix has attempted since rebuilding the game in 2013. Whether splitting the playerbase between Reborn and Evolved rotations — and gating the Switch 2 audience behind a separate subscription — pays off will be measurable from launch month onward.
